chapter_context.summary: Paul describes his heavenly vision and the 'thorn in the flesh' that keeps him humble and reliant on God's grace.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Paul's Vision and His Thorn (12:1-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Corinthians.
This section focuses on Concern for the Corinthian Church (12:11-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Corinthians.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
2 Corinthians 12:1: Paul defends his apostolic integrity and ministry. He highlights the paradox of God's power working through human suffering and weakness.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
We see here the great necessity of humility in the Christian walk. Paul’s earnest desire is for the spiritual prosperity of the believers, reminding them that all blessings flow from the grace of our Lord.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
Analysis of the Greek text reveals Paul's rhetorical strategy. The 'ministry of reconciliation' underscores the legal and relational aspect of the atonement, where God was in Christ reconciling the world to Himself.

key_themes.title: Ministry of Reconciliation
key_themes.description: Our role as ambassadors of Christ to bring the world back to God.

key_themes.title: Strength in Weakness
key_themes.description: God's power is made perfect in our human limitations.

devotional.question: How does this passage speak to your local church community today?

cultural_historical.title: Corinthian Culture
cultural_historical.text: Corinth was a major Greek port city known for its wealth, diversity, and moral challenges, all of which influenced the church's struggles.
